notes: Inhibitor decreases target activity. Directional predicate preferred over the vague parent per Biolink guidance.
Functionally equivalent to inhibitor in this context; collapsed to the same predicate. Distinction recorded here rather than in the graph. See mapping_decisions.md.
Same direction as agonist; two source terms collapsed to one predicate.
Potentiates/enhances the target effect; treated as a positive-direction relationship.
Direction unknown by definition. Mapped up to the vague parent, which Biolink guidance reserves for exactly this case (direction unknown or both).
Unlike plain modulator, this carries direction, so it maps to the directional predicate rather than the vague parent.
Pure binding with no functional direction. Biolink's own example maps 'binds' to physically_interacts_with.
Describes a mechanism, not a clean regulatory direction; mapped to the broad affects parent.
Source admits no known mechanism. Mapped to the most general interaction predicate for drug-gene edges rather than dropped.
This is a drug modality, not an interaction type. Treated as a source data-quality issue and excluded from predicate mapping. See mapping_decisions.md.
Carries positive direction, maps to the directional predicate rather than the vague modulator parent. Mirror of negative modulator.
